# Mars Hydro TSL2000 LED Cannabis Grow Setup & Tutorial (300W LED)
_Last updated Feb 27, 2026_
by Nebula Haze
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/mars-hydro-tsl2000-300w-led-setup-example-with-expected-cost-and-yields.jpg)
[See Other Examples of Cannabis Growing Setups](https://www.growweedeasy.com/growing-cannabis-setup-examples)
## Mars Hydro TSL-2000 Quantum Board LED Grow Setup for Growing Cannabis
  * **Average Yields:** 8-14 oz ounces
  * **Plants:** Recommended 2-6 plants
  * **Optimal grow space** is a 2’x4’x6′ grow tent
  * **Actual Power Consumption** : About 300W at full power, and can be dimmed down for younger plants or if the temperature is getting too high
  * **How Far to Keep from Plants** : 24″ from the top of plants in the vegetative stage, and 18-24″ away in the flowering stage (watch plants for signs of light stress)

[Mars Hydro TSL2000 LED grow light](https://www.amazon.com/MARS-HYDRO-Spectrum-Hydroponic-Fixtures/dp/B07PLZLKVM?pd_rd_r=20a55ddd-bce6-42ab-ac6a-e4bb75ddc537&pd_rd_wg=mM4zi&pd_rd_w=Lg1ae&pd_rd_i=B07PLZLKVM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=adf111eeccaa6651ecfda6e120b943b9&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) – great seed-to-harvest cannabis grow light.
[](https://www.amazon.com/MARS-HYDRO-Spectrum-Hydroponic-Fixtures/dp/B07PLZLKVM?pd_rd_r=20a55ddd-bce6-42ab-ac6a-e4bb75ddc537&pd_rd_wg=mM4zi&pd_rd_w=Lg1ae&pd_rd_i=B07PLZLKVM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=adf111eeccaa6651ecfda6e120b943b9&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
Example of cannabis plants grown under the TSL2000 LED grow light (by Sandra, an alumni of our [Home Grow Masterclass](https://homegrowmasterclass.com/course)).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/incredible-flowering-plants-using-led-grow-lights-by-sandra.jpg)
### About the Mars Hydro TSL2000 LED Setup for Growing Weed Indoors
This is currently the LED I’m using for growing weed. Mars Hydro does testing on live cannabis plants and in shows. Cannabis plants grow fast and healthy under every Mars Hydro LED grow light I’ve tried, and it gets some of the best LED yields.
_Pros:_ The Mars Hydro TSL-2000 is a bar style quantum board with a great spectrum and ultra-high efficiency chips that seems to get better yields than some other LEDs for the amount of electricity. They’re gentle on plants and easy to use.
_Cons:_ More expensive than some of the similar wattage generic LED grow lights available.
### Setup Cost Estimates
**Estimated Cost: $575+**
  * Ultra budget (no smell filtering): $575
  * Ultra budget + smell filtering: $675
  * Premium – higher quality fan plus optional extras: $850

Summary of what’s needed for this [Mars Hydro TSL2000 LED](https://www.amazon.com/MARS-HYDRO-Spectrum-Hydroponic-Fixtures/dp/B07PLZLKVM?pd_rd_r=20a55ddd-bce6-42ab-ac6a-e4bb75ddc537&pd_rd_wg=mM4zi&pd_rd_w=Lg1ae&pd_rd_i=B07PLZLKVM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=adf111eeccaa6651ecfda6e120b943b9&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) cannabis grow light setup.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/mars-hydro-tsl2000-led-grow-tent-shopping-list-for-growing-indoor-weed.jpg)
* * *
## Complete Grow Supply List
This cannabis grow room setup guide with shopping list includes all the supplies you need to get to harvest.
### Mars Hydro TSL2000 Quantum Board LED Grow Light (up to 350 watts)
[**Mars Hydro TSL2000 LED grow light**](https://www.amazon.com/MARS-HYDRO-Spectrum-Hydroponic-Fixtures/dp/B07PLZLKVM?pd_rd_r=20a55ddd-bce6-42ab-ac6a-e4bb75ddc537&pd_rd_wg=mM4zi&pd_rd_w=Lg1ae&pd_rd_i=B07PLZLKVM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=adf111eeccaa6651ecfda6e120b943b9&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)(designed to be used from seed to harvest)
  * Yields: 8-14 oz per harvest
  * Simple to use and hang. Works exactly as you’d expect.
  * Gets great growth on young plants as well as excellent bud formation.
  * Only uses 300 watts of electricity (can be turned down if necessary)

Cost: $360
The [Mars Hydro TSL2000](https://www.amazon.com/MARS-HYDRO-Spectrum-Hydroponic-Fixtures/dp/B07PLZLKVM?pd_rd_r=20a55ddd-bce6-42ab-ac6a-e4bb75ddc537&pd_rd_wg=mM4zi&pd_rd_w=Lg1ae&pd_rd_i=B07PLZLKVM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=adf111eeccaa6651ecfda6e120b943b9&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) can produce up to 14 oz of weed when used as directed
[](https://www.amazon.com/MARS-HYDRO-Spectrum-Hydroponic-Fixtures/dp/B07PLZLKVM?pd_rd_r=20a55ddd-bce6-42ab-ac6a-e4bb75ddc537&pd_rd_wg=mM4zi&pd_rd_w=Lg1ae&pd_rd_i=B07PLZLKVM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=adf111eeccaa6651ecfda6e120b943b9&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
The TSL2000 LED grow light is dimmable, so you have full control over the intensity.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/max-power-mars-hydro-led-grow-light.jpg)
Example of cannabis buds I grew under the Mars Hydro TSL-2000 (strain is [Zweet Inzanity](https://www.northatlanticseed.com/product/zweet-inzanity-rbx-f/ref/63951/)). Check out the [full grow journal](https://www.growweedeasy.com/led-grow-journal-mars-hydro-tsl-2000w-vs-spider-farmer-sf-2000)!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/zweet-inzanity-strain-trichome-bud-mars-hydro-led.jpg)
More cannabis plants grown under the Mars Hydro TSL-2000.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/sandra-cannabis-harvest-mars-hydro-led-grow-light.jpg)
My most recent harvest of cannabis plants grown under the Mars Hydro TSL2000.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/mars-hydro-led-harvest-drying-in-tent.jpg)
**Grow Tent (recommended sizes)**
Just about any grow tent brand works great, but I’ve had good luck with the CoolGrows tents and they’re pretty reasonably priced. A grow tent is an easy and cheap way to instantly create a perfect cannabis grow space that can deal with water, light, heat, and smells.
  * **2’x4’x6′ tent** – an example is [Mars Hydro 48”x24”x71” grow tent](https://www.amazon.com/MarsHydro-Reflective-Hydroponic-MARS-HYDRO/dp/B06Y6FK18S?crid=1DUK6KVJN2SZU&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.13Llz5WE9lxOaO9ni-8V_rPVoubyToELFxU9JzLWfULv9EtnFgCmZ4cZgcm36k-h_mYRHmbnLnzE0Z9uqxE1GdJ2XyL7tAjffgmkUdrTMLYPaCRJpywGXPnwBPym0-Y2yiO01lT9uxf1qGqxUVSAoc7jGXQuMVMCCYeU7IW3fX7O8_3MR4runUDb6g58XDIbpZpfx7xjoJ3BMcZMMRlGyvGk1neXkVhTFitYwRIw7f9cqTGtFy_xcKUCtHp-EeGBEeQ_vB-Z1uFHjcN4tuBRbQjUJjlUrwyJbFPCntrTq0A.Ivqwqv0jQjhVz5TvbyarESA5I5Cuo2YjN21Aw0Oa-tk&dib_tag=se&keywords=2x4x6%2Bgrow%2Btent&qid=1726898877&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=2x4x6%2Clawngarden%2C139&sr=1-4&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=dc87542e53482f1c2cdf7cb83b70868a&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl).

Cost: $110
[This Mars Hydro tent](https://www.amazon.com/MarsHydro-Reflective-Hydroponic-MARS-HYDRO/dp/B06Y6FK18S?crid=1DUK6KVJN2SZU&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.13Llz5WE9lxOaO9ni-8V_rPVoubyToELFxU9JzLWfULv9EtnFgCmZ4cZgcm36k-h_mYRHmbnLnzE0Z9uqxE1GdJ2XyL7tAjffgmkUdrTMLYPaCRJpywGXPnwBPym0-Y2yiO01lT9uxf1qGqxUVSAoc7jGXQuMVMCCYeU7IW3fX7O8_3MR4runUDb6g58XDIbpZpfx7xjoJ3BMcZMMRlGyvGk1neXkVhTFitYwRIw7f9cqTGtFy_xcKUCtHp-EeGBEeQ_vB-Z1uFHjcN4tuBRbQjUJjlUrwyJbFPCntrTq0A.Ivqwqv0jQjhVz5TvbyarESA5I5Cuo2YjN21Aw0Oa-tk&dib_tag=se&keywords=2x4x6%2Bgrow%2Btent&qid=1726898877&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=2x4x6%2Clawngarden%2C139&sr=1-4&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=dc87542e53482f1c2cdf7cb83b70868a&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) is a good fit for the Mars Hydro TSL2000 LED grow light.
[](https://www.amazon.com/MarsHydro-Reflective-Hydroponic-MARS-HYDRO/dp/B06Y6FK18S?crid=1DUK6KVJN2SZU&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.13Llz5WE9lxOaO9ni-8V_rPVoubyToELFxU9JzLWfULv9EtnFgCmZ4cZgcm36k-h_mYRHmbnLnzE0Z9uqxE1GdJ2XyL7tAjffgmkUdrTMLYPaCRJpywGXPnwBPym0-Y2yiO01lT9uxf1qGqxUVSAoc7jGXQuMVMCCYeU7IW3fX7O8_3MR4runUDb6g58XDIbpZpfx7xjoJ3BMcZMMRlGyvGk1neXkVhTFitYwRIw7f9cqTGtFy_xcKUCtHp-EeGBEeQ_vB-Z1uFHjcN4tuBRbQjUJjlUrwyJbFPCntrTq0A.Ivqwqv0jQjhVz5TvbyarESA5I5Cuo2YjN21Aw0Oa-tk&dib_tag=se&keywords=2x4x6%2Bgrow%2Btent&qid=1726898877&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=2x4x6%2Clawngarden%2C139&sr=1-4&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=dc87542e53482f1c2cdf7cb83b70868a&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**6″ Exhaust Fan**
An exhaust fan vents hot air and can be combined with a carbon filter to stop all smells from leaving the grow space. The 6″ size is more than enough for a 2’x4’x6′ grow tent.
  * _Recommended:_ 6″ AC Infinity CLOUDLINE series of inline exhaust fans. These are quiet at top speed and completely silent at lower speeds.
    * [S6 is the cheaper, simpler version](https://www.amazon.com/AC-Infinity-CLOUDLINE-S6-Controller/dp/B07FPFVZTZ/ref=as_li_ss_tl?keywords=AC+Infinity+CLOUDLINE+T6&qid=1549751902&s=gateway&sr=8-2&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=b7afa3ba01b1b757fcacab0d020bc557&language=en_US) which works well and gives you basic fan speed controls
    * [The programmable T6 version](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B074XBXFPD/ref=as_li_ss_tl?psc=1&pd_rd_i=B074XBXFPD&pd_rd_w=LNddZ&pf_rd_p=45e679f6-d55f-4626-99ea-f1ec7720af94&pd_rd_wg=X9Mn6&pf_rd_r=0YN32HB4Q43XQRPHDEJP&pd_rd_r=f87f2b58-5216-4ef2-bd36-7a92f1d91911&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUFZRFNQRzFZUjBaOVYmZW5jcnlwdGVkSWQ9QTA1NDcxNDY3Q0kwRkxHSk9aMUomZW5jcnlwdGVkQWRJZD1BMDQ2Nzc4MjhKTVZaQldMRUVVQSZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2RldGFpbCZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU%3D&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=5f00cae805ce974ffa4de9bf5cb9f3d7&language=en_US) has handy extras like a Temperature/Humidity Probe and Display Screen
    * I picked the 6″ fans because their 4″ fan struggles to keep this size tent cool if the grow room gets warm, though it could get the job done in a pinch.
  * _Budget choice_ (Cheap but loud): 6″ Vivosun fan
    * The [Vivosun 6″ Fan – 440 CFM](https://www.amazon.com/VIVOSUN-Inline-Ventilation-Variable-Controller/dp/B01CTM0JF2?ie=UTF8&qid=1543023460&sr=8-8&keywords=4%2Binch%2Binline%2Bduct%2Bfan&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=f4a049d5c7d10c644d3b954cd9bf694a&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) is significantly smaller and cheaper. However, it is also significantly louder and uses more electricity.

Cost: $80+ ($80-160)
The AC Infinity [CLOUDLINE S6](https://www.amazon.com/AC-Infinity-CLOUDLINE-S6-Controller/dp/B07FPFVZTZ/ref=as_li_ss_tl?keywords=AC+Infinity+CLOUDLINE+T6&qid=1549751902&s=gateway&sr=8-2&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=b7afa3ba01b1b757fcacab0d020bc557&language=en_US) 6″ exhaust fan uses less electricity and runs quieter than most other exhaust fans of similar power. It is silent on the lower settings. The[ programmable T6 version](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B074XBXFPD/ref=as_li_ss_tl?psc=1&pd_rd_i=B074XBXFPD&pd_rd_w=LNddZ&pf_rd_p=45e679f6-d55f-4626-99ea-f1ec7720af94&pd_rd_wg=X9Mn6&pf_rd_r=0YN32HB4Q43XQRPHDEJP&pd_rd_r=f87f2b58-5216-4ef2-bd36-7a92f1d91911&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUFZRFNQRzFZUjBaOVYmZW5jcnlwdGVkSWQ9QTA1NDcxNDY3Q0kwRkxHSk9aMUomZW5jcnlwdGVkQWRJZD1BMDQ2Nzc4MjhKTVZaQldMRUVVQSZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2RldGFpbCZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU%3D&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=5f00cae805ce974ffa4de9bf5cb9f3d7&language=en_US) is the exact same fan but has a digital screen that gives you more control over the fan settings.
[](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B074XBXFPD/ref=as_li_ss_tl?psc=1&pd_rd_i=B074XBXFPD&pd_rd_w=LNddZ&pf_rd_p=45e679f6-d55f-4626-99ea-f1ec7720af94&pd_rd_wg=X9Mn6&pf_rd_r=0YN32HB4Q43XQRPHDEJP&pd_rd_r=f87f2b58-5216-4ef2-bd36-7a92f1d91911&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUFZRFNQRzFZUjBaOVYmZW5jcnlwdGVkSWQ9QTA1NDcxNDY3Q0kwRkxHSk9aMUomZW5jcnlwdGVkQWRJZD1BMDQ2Nzc4MjhKTVZaQldMRUVVQSZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2RldGFpbCZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU%3D&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=5f00cae805ce974ffa4de9bf5cb9f3d7&language=en_US)
**(Optional) Carbon Filter – For smell filtering**
When set up properly, a carbon filter removes 100% of odors but it must be attached to a good exhaust fan (like the AC Infinity above) to work.
  * _Recommended:_ [AC Infinity 6-inch Carbon Filter, 6″ x 16″ version](https://www.amazon.com/AC-Infinity-Australian-Charcoal-Hydroponics/dp/B07GSKTT86?pd_rd_w=ORxa0&content-id=amzn1.sym.9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_p=9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_r=XZ4XJ9XV4XTE4FF3YX6M&pd_rd_wg=menIM&pd_rd_r=74b589d8-3351-43ba-b571-840d9e9ea446&pd_rd_i=B07GSKTT86&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=4d0200fb4130273781a427f9dc53fd30&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) plus [ducting/clamps](https://www.amazon.com/AC-Infinity-Heavy-Duty-Four-Layer-Ventilation/dp/B0791V19H7?pd_rd_w=akKOh&content-id=amzn1.sym.7757a8b5-874e-4a67-9d85-54ed32f01737&pf_rd_p=7757a8b5-874e-4a67-9d85-54ed32f01737&pf_rd_r=NZG90YCJWTKP1JHBZG4Y&pd_rd_wg=sALM2&pd_rd_r=b91d8930-56c4-47e5-927d-a9068cc41e1b&pd_rd_i=B0791V19H7&psc=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=2c5bc94fe3f9ecb93c12a22db0c1a060&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)

Cost: $100
I’ve had great smell filtering with the above 6″ AC Infinity Cloudline fan attached to a matching [6″ carbon filter](https://www.amazon.com/AC-Infinity-Australian-Charcoal-Hydroponics/dp/B07GSKTT86?pd_rd_w=ORxa0&content-id=amzn1.sym.9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_p=9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_r=XZ4XJ9XV4XTE4FF3YX6M&pd_rd_wg=menIM&pd_rd_r=74b589d8-3351-43ba-b571-840d9e9ea446&pd_rd_i=B07GSKTT86&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=4d0200fb4130273781a427f9dc53fd30&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) with [6″ ducting](https://www.amazon.com/AC-Infinity-Heavy-Duty-Four-Layer-Ventilation/dp/B0791V19H7?pd_rd_w=akKOh&content-id=amzn1.sym.7757a8b5-874e-4a67-9d85-54ed32f01737&pf_rd_p=7757a8b5-874e-4a67-9d85-54ed32f01737&pf_rd_r=NZG90YCJWTKP1JHBZG4Y&pd_rd_wg=sALM2&pd_rd_r=b91d8930-56c4-47e5-927d-a9068cc41e1b&pd_rd_i=B0791V19H7&psc=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=2c5bc94fe3f9ecb93c12a22db0c1a060&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl).
[](https://www.amazon.com/AC-Infinity-Australian-Charcoal-Hydroponics/dp/B07GSKTT86?pd_rd_w=ORxa0&content-id=amzn1.sym.9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_p=9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_r=XZ4XJ9XV4XTE4FF3YX6M&pd_rd_wg=menIM&pd_rd_r=74b589d8-3351-43ba-b571-840d9e9ea446&pd_rd_i=B07GSKTT86&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=6c4f8472c1d528a296f4dc209348a6e0&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**Plant Pots**
I prefer fabric pots or air pots because cannabis plants grow faster but need to be watered more often than standard hard-sided plant pots. Both types work great for growing cannabis indoors. Often the best plant pot is whatever is cheap and easy for you to get.
  * Recommended: [Fabric pots](https://www.amazon.com/VIVOSUN-10-Pack-Reinforced-Planter-Gardening/dp/B08HRR1ZJM?pd_rd_w=mXh9T&content-id=amzn1.sym.9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_p=9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_r=89CR8VM80YPZSJ58PV3J&pd_rd_wg=w0OgQ&pd_rd_r=a62f2630-ffff-4d23-839e-c73fc37d70e1&pd_rd_i=B08HRR1ZJM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=7beb935e289005969327e3138cd535e9&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) between 2-7 gallons (any fabric pots work great so I pick whatever is cheap or looks the best to me)

Cost: $20
These [5-gallon fabric pots](https://www.amazon.com/VIVOSUN-10-Pack-Reinforced-Planter-Gardening/dp/B08HRTY8PY?pd_rd_w=mXh9T&content-id=amzn1.sym.9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_p=9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_r=89CR8VM80YPZSJ58PV3J&pd_rd_wg=w0OgQ&pd_rd_r=a62f2630-ffff-4d23-839e-c73fc37d70e1&pd_rd_i=B08HRR1ZJM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=624ac469732f0f838ab9ddab983f0f0c&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) work great in this setup.
[](https://www.amazon.com/VIVOSUN-10-Pack-Reinforced-Planter-Gardening/dp/B08HRTY8PY?pd_rd_w=mXh9T&content-id=amzn1.sym.9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_p=9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_r=89CR8VM80YPZSJ58PV3J&pd_rd_wg=w0OgQ&pd_rd_r=a62f2630-ffff-4d23-839e-c73fc37d70e1&pd_rd_i=B08HRR1ZJM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=624ac469732f0f838ab9ddab983f0f0c&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**Grow Medium (Soil, Coco, Hydro)**
Grow mediums can include coco coir or soil (hydroponics for more advanced growers) but if you’re not sure, a coco-based soil like Coco Loco is perfect for beginners.
  * Recommended Soil: One 2 cu ft bag of [coco-based soil with perlite](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW?crid=24PW79IKBPTBM&keywords=coco+loco&qid=1654831935&s=home-garden&sprefix=coco+loco%2Cgarden%2C130&sr=1-2&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=c830226c3934b8206efad9422957fa1d&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) should be able to fill 15 gallons worth of pots. For example, it will fill 3 x [5-gallon fabric pots](https://www.amazon.com/VIVOSUN-10-Pack-Reinforced-Planter-Gardening/dp/B08HRTY8PY?pd_rd_w=mXh9T&content-id=amzn1.sym.9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_p=9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_r=89CR8VM80YPZSJ58PV3J&pd_rd_wg=w0OgQ&pd_rd_r=a62f2630-ffff-4d23-839e-c73fc37d70e1&pd_rd_i=B08HRR1ZJM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=624ac469732f0f838ab9ddab983f0f0c&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl).

Cost: $40
[Coco Loco](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW?crid=24PW79IKBPTBM&keywords=coco+loco&qid=1654831935&s=home-garden&sprefix=coco+loco%2Cgarden%2C130&sr=1-2&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=c830226c3934b8206efad9422957fa1d&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) is a coco-based rich airy soil that cannabis plants thrive in.
[](https://www.amazon.com/Fox-Farm-FBA_FX14100-Potting-Cubic/dp/B00HP30LWW?crid=24PW79IKBPTBM&keywords=coco+loco&qid=1654831935&s=home-garden&sprefix=coco+loco%2Cgarden%2C130&sr=1-2&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=c830226c3934b8206efad9422957fa1d&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**Nutrients**
Many nutrient systems have a nutrient schedule so you give your plants the right ratios for each stage of life. Basically, you use a “vegetative” or all-purpose plant nutrient for the first part of your plant’s life and a “bloom” or low-nitrogen nutrients when buds are forming.  _Tip:_ Always start at half-strength with a new base nutrient system since the recommended amount can be too high for cannabis plants.
**Recommended Beginner Nutrients:[Dyna-Gro Grow + Bloom](https://www.amazon.com/Dyna-Gro-DYNAGB8OZSET-Liquid-Grow-Bloom/dp/B014UAEPVK/ref=as_li_ss_tl?crid=CIE0NFW2OZX4&dchild=1&keywords=dyna+gro&qid=1598131707&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=duna+gro,aps,193&sr=1-6&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=0fde877e0e93fc7fe875464cb3ae2ec4&language=en_US)** nutrient combo is excellent, cheap, and easy to use for growing cannabis indoors.
  * **Dyna-Gro “Grow”** : Give 1 tsp per gallon of water during the vegetative stage.
  * **Dyna-Gro “Bloom”** : Give 1 tsp/gallon of water after buds start forming.

Cost: $20
[Dyna-Gro Grow + Bloom](https://www.amazon.com/Dyna-Gro-DYNAGB8OZSET-Liquid-Grow-Bloom/dp/B014UAEPVK/ref=as_li_ss_tl?crid=CIE0NFW2OZX4&dchild=1&keywords=dyna+gro&qid=1598131707&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=duna+gro,aps,193&sr=1-6&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=0fde877e0e93fc7fe875464cb3ae2ec4&language=en_US) is a cheap and simple nutrient system for newbies. One bottle for veg, one bottle for after buds start forming. 1 tsp/gallon. Super simple!
[](https://www.amazon.com/Dyna-Gro-DYNAGB8OZSET-Liquid-Grow-Bloom/dp/B014UAEPVK/ref=as_li_ss_tl?crid=CIE0NFW2OZX4&dchild=1&keywords=dyna+gro&qid=1598131707&s=lawn-garden&sprefix=duna+gro,aps,193&sr=1-6&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=0fde877e0e93fc7fe875464cb3ae2ec4&language=en_US)
_Premium Nutrient Alternative:_[General Hydroponics Flora Trio](https://www.amazon.com/Hydroponics-GH-Flora-FloraMicro-FloraBloom/dp/B01HG98GX0?_encoding=UTF8&pd_rd_w=X1HVZ&content-id=amzn1.sym.255b3518-6e7f-495c-8611-30a58648072e%3Aamzn1.symc.a68f4ca3-28dc-4388-a2cf-24672c480d8f&pf_rd_p=255b3518-6e7f-495c-8611-30a58648072e&pf_rd_r=EFYFDMHBHVZSJEJEHG45&pd_rd_wg=Gv5WE&pd_rd_r=19c9e32b-29c4-4de8-90df-b09c269c3f64&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=81d21b403897cfd816707ad2e813a0ec&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl). It costs a bit more and isn’t quite as simple as Dyna-Gro, but this is my personal favorite nutrient system for growing weed. Check out my [cannabis growers’ guide to the Flora trio](https://www.growweedeasy.com/general-hydroponics-flora-trio-guide).
[](https://www.amazon.com/Hydroponics-GH-Flora-FloraMicro-FloraBloom/dp/B01HG98GX0?_encoding=UTF8&pd_rd_w=X1HVZ&content-id=amzn1.sym.255b3518-6e7f-495c-8611-30a58648072e%3Aamzn1.symc.a68f4ca3-28dc-4388-a2cf-24672c480d8f&pf_rd_p=255b3518-6e7f-495c-8611-30a58648072e&pf_rd_r=EFYFDMHBHVZSJEJEHG45&pd_rd_wg=Gv5WE&pd_rd_r=19c9e32b-29c4-4de8-90df-b09c269c3f64&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=81d21b403897cfd816707ad2e813a0ec&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**Other Nice-to-Have Stuff**
  * [24-Hour Timer](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B00MVFF59S/ref=as_li_ss_tl?psc=1&pd_rd_i=B00MVFF59S&pf_rd_m=ATVPDKIKX0DER&pf_rd_p=21517efd-b385-405b-a405-9a37af61b5b4&pd_rd_wg=84q6G&pf_rd_r=F1MKWCD8J86C00QTV32T&pf_rd_s=desktop-dp-sims&pf_rd_t=40701&pd_rd_w=kUPzK&pf_rd_i=desktop-dp-sims&pd_rd_r=bc24082b-de5e-11e8-8ab1-ed751b73ebab&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=182f7f9c3bd76c08d99c0c239dd7ec99&language=en_US) (to put your grow lights on a timer so the plants get a day and night period on a schedule) – $10
  * Water containers (I personally use a [3-gallon](https://www.amazon.com/H8O-Polycarbonate-gallon-Stubby-Bottle/dp/B00BU95T92?pd_rd_w=KXIo3&content-id=amzn1.sym.db26482d-f2e6-4f69-a20a-6ff6cd9fa602&pf_rd_p=db26482d-f2e6-4f69-a20a-6ff6cd9fa602&pf_rd_r=91ZRN81S3AK9SFCVH1S0&pd_rd_wg=72P8Z&pd_rd_r=15b4b9c9-a3a1-4c55-a61d-dd4312f8c08a&pd_rd_i=B00BU95T92&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=4414cda26ba71bd1d39adcb894155763&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) or [5-gallon](https://www.amazon.com/Alpine-Cuisine-Plastic-Gallons-Container/dp/B08BSNV68F?pd_rd_w=LgcCD&content-id=amzn1.sym.9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_p=9023959a-379f-4911-bf85-9afa60b85373&pf_rd_r=2ACWQY41G3C6XCWS56TJ&pd_rd_wg=kPbuo&pd_rd_r=399be440-356d-4b3e-a00e-2240be74fdde&pd_rd_i=B08BSNV68F&psc=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=0888f2d8137c4f97c758af26f5268de0&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) water jug to mix up nutrients, but anything can work) – Much cheaper at Wal-Mart ($5-15) than online!
  * Extra [1/8″ Rope Ratchets](https://www.amazon.com/Simple-Deluxe-Pairs-Adjustable-Heavy/dp/B0CR67VMFM?pd_rd_w=gblxD&content-id=amzn1.sym.b3f15950-3c9d-4907-ad05-73533fd419e9&pf_rd_p=b3f15950-3c9d-4907-ad05-73533fd419e9&pf_rd_r=MV5DC5PWD2WTY7P1JD9G&pd_rd_wg=KthlN&pd_rd_r=3a7f5fa8-9045-4466-ba6c-45b611a8bb73&pd_rd_i=B0CR67VMFM&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=7ff33fd873f6aa9d4a968b2c6fb2c9b3&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (sooooo handy) – These can be used to hang your grow light or carbon filter from inside the tent, and still be able to easily move it up and down without needing any strength. Your grow light usually comes with a set of rope ratchets, but it’s a great idea to get an extra set to hang the carbon filter – $10
  * [Thermometer/Humidity Monitor](https://www.amazon.com/Weewooday-Thermometer-Hygrometer-Terrarium-Temperature/dp/B08RS947DG?pd_rd_w=ciqTy&content-id=amzn1.sym.db26482d-f2e6-4f69-a20a-6ff6cd9fa602&pf_rd_p=db26482d-f2e6-4f69-a20a-6ff6cd9fa602&pf_rd_r=2FDM4XA9HB0HR7PG8FFX&pd_rd_wg=zqQmJ&pd_rd_r=e4304fbe-beaf-45d5-aa04-d24713950c61&pd_rd_i=B08RS947DG&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=5406604f67d765f03b7842b42d6c77f7&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) with a probe so you can keep track of the temperature inside the tent – $15
    * Advanced Alternative: These [Govee Thermometers](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0872ZWV8X?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=f5d3199c0b86674e8a75acec313f6294&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) let you check the temperature and humidity from your phone. Convenient! – $32
  * [PH Test Kit](https://www.amazon.com/General-Hydroponics-pH-Control-Kit/dp/B000BNKWZY/ref=as_li_ss_tl?ie=UTF8&qid=1541205640&sr=8-1&keywords=general+hydroponics+ph+control+kit&&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=e3cac12e7b6b284f2813ce8efa58350c&language=en_US) – Prevent potential nutrient deficiencies by testing the water pH – $20
  * [Measuring spoons](https://www.amazon.com/Spring-Chef-Magnetic-Measuring-Stainless/dp/B07DJ53GZQ?&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=3cbc480301b181be09da2ee96996b832&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) for measuring out your nutrients (many people already have these) – $9

**Estimated Cost: $575-$850** plus the cost of seeds
  * Ultra budget (no smell filtering): $575
  * Ultra budget + smell filtering: $675
  * Premium – higher quality fan plus optional extras: $850

GrowWeedEasy.com was [founded in 2010](https://www.growweedeasy.com/about) by Nebula Haze and Sirius Fourside. Featured in _High Times_ , Ed Rosenthal’s books, _Cannabis Business Times_ , and _Skunk Magazine_ , GrowWeedEasy.com is the **#1 free resource in the world** for learning how to grow cannabis at home. Our simple step-by-step tutorials make it easy for anyone to grow great weed. **[Sign up for our free newsletter](https://www.growweedeasy.com/message/news)** to get grow tips delivered every Sunday at 4:20 AM.
